text: The System shall track the acquisition of lapsed quantities by specific permits and wells.
The System shall identify the geographic location of all lapsed quantities.
The System shall record the reason for every lapsed quantity instance including expiration, cancellation, and retirement.
The System shall categorize lapsed quantities by their corresponding MFL zone.
The System shall track the relocation of active water use within the SWUCA caution area.
The System shall generate reports on the history of irrigation water conserving credits.
The System shall identify if expired permits have had quantities reallocated to active permits.
The System shall track alternative source projects including surface water, reclaimed water, and augmented surface water.
The System shall display a map showing the spatial impact of permit applications relative to existing active applications.
The System shall allow users to query permits by geographic area or permit number.
The System shall generate heat maps indicating the over-pumping status of permits based on use type.
The System shall display a heat map for water quality using the District WUPnet sentinel wells.
The System shall calculate the impact of water use on set MFL levels.
The System shall track net benefit changes for relocated permits and changes of use type.
The System shall report net benefit amounts in MGD for land use changes.
The System shall identify permits requiring quantity relocation to public supply utilities due to land use change.
The System shall create a map layer depicting cones of depression for MFL information.
The System shall report on water use amounts transferred between permits.
The System shall report the improvement percentage for mitigation of MFL impacts.
The System shall identify the source of benefit for water resource development projects.
The System shall track permit duration and expiration dates.
The System shall allow comparison of District GIS layers with adjacent water management district layers.
The System shall report compliance at the permit level.
The System shall identify competing applications including those in modification or expiring.
The System shall compare actual water levels to established MFL thresholds.
The System shall determine the availability of alternative supplies including reclaimed water and desalination.
The System shall report the reservation from use amounts allocated to participants.
The System shall track the ownership of withdrawal points over time.
The System shall link permitted quantities to actual pumpage quantities and resource management data.
The System shall identify when standby permits are reactivated due to loss of alternative quantities.
The System shall generate maps showing predominate use types by percentage for specified time periods.
The System shall provide a customizable map printing interface.
The System shall identify permits situated on land purchased by government agencies for retirement.
The System shall track water use changes relative to land use changes.
The System shall provide a permit search interface using issue date, expiration date, and use type.
The System shall generate a well package file for import into Groundwater Vistas modeling software.
The System shall associate permitted quantities and pumpage data at the well level.
The System shall track trends in land use and water use changes over time.
The System shall track withdrawal sources by aquifer or USGS water body.
The System shall display the rolling 12-month average, peak month, and max month pumpage for any selected well.
The System shall compare a permit's over-pumping percentage against the regional average for the same use type.
The System shall export selected water use data to an Excel spreadsheet.
The System shall generate monthly reports showing the difference in quantities permitted per month by geographic area.
The System shall calculate the relationship between rainfall and pumpage data.
The System shall categorize water use as groundwater, surface water, or re-use.
The System shall support spatial queries using graphics or polygon buffering.
The System shall track aquifer level and stream flow changes by area on a monthly basis.
The System shall display a map layer for Developments of Regional Impacts (DRI).
The System shall identify the status of stressed lakes.
The System shall flag permittees with consistent pumpage readings despite missing data.
The System shall identify permittees reporting zero on pumpage reports.
The System shall generate a map of permittees failing to submit required conditional reports.
The System shall report on permits out of compliance due to over-pumping.
The System shall compare the 10-year moving AFL to the MFL for aquifer saltwater intrusion.
The System shall compare Peace River minimum flows to established MFLs over a 3-year period.
The System shall compare long-term P50 and P10 levels to High Minimum Lake Levels.
The System shall publish charts comparing MFL to the running average of AFLs.
The System shall import estimated quantities for unmetered permits from the Water Use Estimates database.
The System shall provide access to population data aggregated by service area.
The System shall provide spatial and temporal access to crop report information.
The System shall link Water Use Permits to Environmental Resource Permits and Well Construction Permits.
The System shall replicate DB2 data to the Oracle database daily.
The System shall maintain data metadata in compliance with FGDC standards.
The System shall display actual data values instead of internal database codes.
The System shall utilize the existing District architecture including Oracle Spatial and ArcSDE.
The System shall provide on-line help and training materials for all users.
The System shall refresh screen displays within a reasonable time frame established by District performance standards.
The System shall support the creation of what-if scenarios for drought events.
The System shall allow users to download query results to external systems.
The System shall adhere to District programming and change management standards.
